fields.name: cloudtrace_projects_traces_batchWrite
fields.description: Batch writes new spans to new or existing traces. You cannot update existing spans. If a span ID already exists, an additional copy of the span will be stored.

fields.name: cloudtrace_projects_traces_spans_createSpan
fields.description: Creates a new span. If a span ID already exists, an additional copy of the span will be stored.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: BatchWriteSpansRequest_Input
types.description: The request message for the `BatchWriteSpans` method.

inputFields.name: spans
inputFields.description: Required. A list of new spans. The span names must not match existing spans, otherwise the results are undefined.

types.name: Span_Input
types.description: A span represents a single operation within a trace. Spans can be nested to form a trace tree. Often, a trace contains a root span that describes the end-to-end latency, and one or more subspans for its sub-operations. A trace can also contain multiple root spans, or none at all. Spans do not need to be contiguous. There might be gaps or overlaps between spans in a trace.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The resource name of the span in the following format: * `projects/[PROJECT_ID]/traces/[TRACE_ID]/spans/[SPAN_ID]` `[TRACE_ID]` is a unique identifier for a trace within a project; it is a 32-character hexadecimal encoding of a 16-byte array. It should not be zero. `[SPAN_ID]` is a unique identifier for a span within a trace; it is a 16-character hexadecimal encoding of an 8-byte array. It should not be zero. .

inputFields.name: spanId
inputFields.description: Required. The `[SPAN_ID]` portion of the span's resource name.

inputFields.name: parentSpanId
inputFields.description: The `[SPAN_ID]` of this span's parent span. If this is a root span, then this field must be empty.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Required. The start time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution starts. On the server side, this is the time when the server's application handler starts running.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: Required. The end time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution ends. On the server side, this is the time when the server application handler stops running.

inputFields.name: sameProcessAsParentSpan
inputFields.description: Optional. Set this parameter to indicate whether this span is in the same process as its parent. If you do not set this parameter, Trace is unable to take advantage of this helpful information.

inputFields.name: childSpanCount
inputFields.description: Optional. The number of child spans that were generated while this span was active. If set, allows implementation to detect missing child spans.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: TruncatableString_Input
types.description: Represents a string that might be shortened to a specified length.

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: The shortened string. For example, if the original string is 500 bytes long and the limit of the string is 128 bytes, then `value` contains the first 128 bytes of the 500-byte string. Truncation always happens on a UTF8 character boundary. If there are multi-byte characters in the string, then the length of the shortened string might be less than the size limit.

inputFields.name: truncatedByteCount
inputFields.description: The number of bytes removed from the original string. If this value is 0, then the string was not shortened.

types.name: Attributes_Input
types.description: A set of attributes as key-value pairs.

inputFields.name: droppedAttributesCount
inputFields.description: The number of attributes that were discarded. Attributes can be discarded because their keys are too long or because there are too many attributes. If this value is 0 then all attributes are valid.

types.name: StackTrace_Input
types.description: A call stack appearing in a trace.

inputFields.name: stackTraceHashId
inputFields.description: The hash ID is used to conserve network bandwidth for duplicate stack traces within a single trace. Often multiple spans will have identical stack traces. The first occurrence of a stack trace should contain both the `stackFrame` content and a value in `stackTraceHashId`. Subsequent spans within the same request can refer to that stack trace by only setting `stackTraceHashId`.

types.name: StackFrames_Input
types.description: A collection of stack frames, which can be truncated.

inputFields.name: frame
inputFields.description: Stack frames in this call stack.

inputFields.name: droppedFramesCount
inputFields.description: The number of stack frames that were dropped because there were too many stack frames. If this value is 0, then no stack frames were dropped.

types.name: StackFrame_Input
types.description: Represents a single stack frame in a stack trace.

inputFields.name: lineNumber
inputFields.description: The line number in `file_name` where the function call appears.

inputFields.name: columnNumber
inputFields.description: The column number where the function call appears, if available. This is important in JavaScript because of its anonymous functions.

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: Module_Input
types.description: Binary module.

types.name: TimeEvents_Input
types.description: A collection of `TimeEvent`s. A `TimeEvent` is a time-stamped annotation on the span, consisting of either user-supplied key:value pairs, or details of a message sent/received between Spans.

inputFields.name: timeEvent
inputFields.description: A collection of `TimeEvent`s.

inputFields.name: droppedAnnotationsCount
inputFields.description: The number of dropped annotations in all the included time events. If the value is 0, then no annotations were dropped.

inputFields.name: droppedMessageEventsCount
inputFields.description: The number of dropped message events in all the included time events. If the value is 0, then no message events were dropped.

types.name: TimeEvent_Input
types.description: A time-stamped annotation or message event in the Span.

inputFields.name: time
inputFields.description: The timestamp indicating the time the event occurred.

types.name: Annotation_Input
types.description: Text annotation with a set of attributes.

types.name: MessageEvent_Input
types.description: An event describing a message sent/received between Spans.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: An identifier for the MessageEvent's message that can be used to match `SENT` and `RECEIVED` MessageEvents.

inputFields.name: uncompressedSizeBytes
inputFields.description: The number of uncompressed bytes sent or received.

inputFields.name: compressedSizeBytes
inputFields.description: The number of compressed bytes sent or received. If missing, the compressed size is assumed to be the same size as the uncompressed size.

types.name: mutation_cloudtrace_projects_traces_spans_createSpan_timeEvents_timeEvent_items_messageEvent_type
types.description: Type of MessageEvent. Indicates whether the message was sent or received.

types.name: Links_Input
types.description: A collection of links, which are references from this span to a span in the same or different trace.

inputFields.name: link
inputFields.description: A collection of links.

inputFields.name: droppedLinksCount
inputFields.description: The number of dropped links after the maximum size was enforced. If this value is 0, then no links were dropped.

types.name: Link_Input
types.description: A pointer from the current span to another span in the same trace or in a different trace. For example, this can be used in batching operations, where a single batch handler processes multiple requests from different traces or when the handler receives a request from a different project.

inputFields.name: traceId
inputFields.description: The `[TRACE_ID]` for a trace within a project.

inputFields.name: spanId
inputFields.description: The `[SPAN_ID]` for a span within a trace.

types.name: mutation_cloudtrace_projects_traces_spans_createSpan_links_link_items_type
types.description: The relationship of the current span relative to the linked span.

types.name: Status_Input
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

inputFields.name: code
inputFields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

inputFields.name: details
inputFields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: mutation_cloudtrace_projects_traces_spans_createSpan_spanKind
types.description: Optional. Distinguishes between spans generated in a particular context. For example, two spans with the same name may be distinguished using `CLIENT` (caller) and `SERVER` (callee) to identify an RPC call.

types.name: Span
types.description: A span represents a single operation within a trace. Spans can be nested to form a trace tree. Often, a trace contains a root span that describes the end-to-end latency, and one or more subspans for its sub-operations. A trace can also contain multiple root spans, or none at all. Spans do not need to be contiguous. There might be gaps or overlaps between spans in a trace.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the span in the following format: * `projects/[PROJECT_ID]/traces/[TRACE_ID]/spans/[SPAN_ID]` `[TRACE_ID]` is a unique identifier for a trace within a project; it is a 32-character hexadecimal encoding of a 16-byte array. It should not be zero. `[SPAN_ID]` is a unique identifier for a span within a trace; it is a 16-character hexadecimal encoding of an 8-byte array. It should not be zero. .

fields.name: spanId
fields.description: Required. The `[SPAN_ID]` portion of the span's resource name.

fields.name: parentSpanId
fields.description: The `[SPAN_ID]` of this span's parent span. If this is a root span, then this field must be empty.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Required. The start time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution starts. On the server side, this is the time when the server's application handler starts running.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Required. The end time of the span. On the client side, this is the time kept by the local machine where the span execution ends. On the server side, this is the time when the server application handler stops running.

fields.name: sameProcessAsParentSpan
fields.description: Optional. Set this parameter to indicate whether this span is in the same process as its parent. If you do not set this parameter, Trace is unable to take advantage of this helpful information.

fields.name: childSpanCount
fields.description: Optional. The number of child spans that were generated while this span was active. If set, allows implementation to detect missing child spans.

types.name: TruncatableString
types.description: Represents a string that might be shortened to a specified length.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The shortened string. For example, if the original string is 500 bytes long and the limit of the string is 128 bytes, then `value` contains the first 128 bytes of the 500-byte string. Truncation always happens on a UTF8 character boundary. If there are multi-byte characters in the string, then the length of the shortened string might be less than the size limit.

fields.name: truncatedByteCount
fields.description: The number of bytes removed from the original string. If this value is 0, then the string was not shortened.

types.name: Attributes
types.description: A set of attributes as key-value pairs.

fields.name: droppedAttributesCount
fields.description: The number of attributes that were discarded. Attributes can be discarded because their keys are too long or because there are too many attributes. If this value is 0 then all attributes are valid.

types.name: mutation_cloudtrace_projects_traces_spans_createSpan_attributes_attributeMap
types.description: A set of attributes. Each attribute's key can be up to 128 bytes long. The value can be a string up to 256 bytes, a signed 64-bit integer, or the boolean values `true` or `false`. For example: "/instance_id": { "string_value": { "value": "my-instance" } } "/http/request_bytes": { "int_value": 300 } "example.com/myattribute": { "bool_value": false }

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: AttributeValue
types.description: The allowed types for `[VALUE]` in a `[KEY]:[VALUE]` attribute.

fields.name: intValue
fields.description: A 64-bit signed integer.

fields.name: boolValue
fields.description: A Boolean value represented by `true` or `false`.

types.name: StackTrace
types.description: A call stack appearing in a trace.

fields.name: stackTraceHashId
fields.description: The hash ID is used to conserve network bandwidth for duplicate stack traces within a single trace. Often multiple spans will have identical stack traces. The first occurrence of a stack trace should contain both the `stackFrame` content and a value in `stackTraceHashId`. Subsequent spans within the same request can refer to that stack trace by only setting `stackTraceHashId`.

types.name: StackFrames
types.description: A collection of stack frames, which can be truncated.

fields.name: frame
fields.description: Stack frames in this call stack.

fields.name: droppedFramesCount
fields.description: The number of stack frames that were dropped because there were too many stack frames. If this value is 0, then no stack frames were dropped.

types.name: StackFrame
types.description: Represents a single stack frame in a stack trace.

fields.name: lineNumber
fields.description: The line number in `file_name` where the function call appears.

fields.name: columnNumber
fields.description: The column number where the function call appears, if available. This is important in JavaScript because of its anonymous functions.

types.name: Module
types.description: Binary module.

types.name: TimeEvents
types.description: A collection of `TimeEvent`s. A `TimeEvent` is a time-stamped annotation on the span, consisting of either user-supplied key:value pairs, or details of a message sent/received between Spans.

fields.name: timeEvent
fields.description: A collection of `TimeEvent`s.

fields.name: droppedAnnotationsCount
fields.description: The number of dropped annotations in all the included time events. If the value is 0, then no annotations were dropped.

fields.name: droppedMessageEventsCount
fields.description: The number of dropped message events in all the included time events. If the value is 0, then no message events were dropped.

types.name: TimeEvent
types.description: A time-stamped annotation or message event in the Span.

fields.name: time
fields.description: The timestamp indicating the time the event occurred.

types.name: Annotation
types.description: Text annotation with a set of attributes.

types.name: MessageEvent
types.description: An event describing a message sent/received between Spans.

fields.name: id
fields.description: An identifier for the MessageEvent's message that can be used to match `SENT` and `RECEIVED` MessageEvents.

fields.name: uncompressedSizeBytes
fields.description: The number of uncompressed bytes sent or received.

fields.name: compressedSizeBytes
fields.description: The number of compressed bytes sent or received. If missing, the compressed size is assumed to be the same size as the uncompressed size.

types.name: Links
types.description: A collection of links, which are references from this span to a span in the same or different trace.

fields.name: link
fields.description: A collection of links.

fields.name: droppedLinksCount
fields.description: The number of dropped links after the maximum size was enforced. If this value is 0, then no links were dropped.

types.name: Link
types.description: A pointer from the current span to another span in the same trace or in a different trace. For example, this can be used in batching operations, where a single batch handler processes multiple requests from different traces or when the handler receives a request from a different project.

fields.name: traceId
fields.description: The `[TRACE_ID]` for a trace within a project.

fields.name: spanId
fields.description: The `[SPAN_ID]` for a span within a trace.

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

fields.name: details
fields.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
